text: IRD Sri Lanka Tax Chart for Year of Assessment 2025/2026. The source page covers tax rates for resident and non-resident individuals, terminal benefits from employment, companies, remittance tax, partnerships, trusts, unit trusts or mutual funds, non-governmental organizations, charitable institutions, employee and pension-related funds, withholding tax and advanced income tax, advance personal income tax, value added tax, social security contribution levy, stamp duty, share transaction levy, and betting and gaming levy.

section: Tax Rates for Resident and Non-Resident Individuals
subsection: Available reliefs and progressive income slabs
text: Year of Assessment 2025/2026. Available reliefs for individuals: personal relief for residents and non-resident citizens of Sri Lanka is Rs. 1,800,000 and is not available for gains from the realization of investment assets; rent relief for a resident individual's investment assets is 25% of the total rental income for the year of assessment, depending on the total rent income of the respective period; a resident individual who has acquired solar panels fixed on his premises and connected to the national grid may claim Rs. 600,000 for each year of assessment, up to the total expenditure on the solar panels or up to amounts paid to a bank in respect of a loan obtained to acquire the solar panels. Taxable income rates: first Rs. 1,000,000 at 6%; next Rs. 500,000 at 18%; next Rs. 500,000 at 24%; next Rs. 500,000 at 30%; balance at 36%.

section: Tax Rates for Resident and Non-Resident Individuals
text: Special rates for individuals: gains from the realization of investment assets are taxed at 10%; income from a business consisting of betting and gaming, manufacture and sale, or import and sale of any liquor or tobacco product is taxed at 45%; gains and profits earned or derived from services rendered in or outside Sri Lanka to any person for use outside Sri Lanka, where payment is received in foreign currency and remitted through a bank to Sri Lanka, are taxed at a maximum rate of 15%; gains and profits earned or derived from any foreign source, where such gains and profits are earned in foreign currency and remitted through a bank to Sri Lanka, are taxed at a maximum rate of 15%.

section: Tax on Once-and-for-all Payments / Terminal Benefits from Employment
subsection: Types of terminal benefits and exemptions
text: Types of terminal benefits or income from employment listed on the page: an amount received in commutation of a pension; an amount received as a retiring gratuity; an amount received as compensation for loss of office or employment under a scheme that the Commissioner General considers uniformly applicable to all individuals employed by the employer; an amount paid at or after retirement from employment from the Employee's Trust Fund established by the Employees' Trust Fund Act No. 46 of 1980; and any other payments or benefits received in respect of retirement. The page notes that employers should obtain Commissioner General approval for a compensation for loss of office scheme by contacting the Tax Policy and Legislation Unit before the scheme commences. Exemptions: any amount received from a Provident Fund approved by the Commissioner General of Inland Revenue or regulated provident funds; capital sums paid as compensation or gratuity in relation to personal injuries suffered by the person or the death of another person; and pension or any retiring benefit paid by the Government or a Department of Government of Sri Lanka. The page also refers readers to APIT Table No. 03 on the IRD web portal for further information.

section: Tax on Once-and-for-all Payments / Terminal Benefits from Employment
text: Application of concessionary rates on terminal benefits from employment: first Rs. 10,000,000 of the total qualifying income from employment is taxed at 0%; next Rs. 10,000,000 is taxed at 6%; balance is taxed at 12%.

section: Tax on Once-and-for-all Payments / Terminal Benefits from Employment
text: Application of normal rates on terminal benefits from employment applies to compensation for loss of office not approved by the Commissioner General of Inland Revenue, payment from a provident fund that is not a regulated fund or not approved by the Commissioner General, other payments, or any retirement non-cash benefit. The page states these are subject to normal rates up to the maximum rate. Taxable income rates: first Rs. 1,000,000 at 6%; next Rs. 500,000 at 18%; next Rs. 500,000 at 24%; next Rs. 500,000 at 30%; balance at 36%.

Company tax rates. Standard rate: taxable income of a company, other than companies taxed at special rates, is taxed at 30%. Gains and profits earned or derived from services rendered in or outside Sri Lanka to any person for use outside Sri Lanka, where payment is received in foreign currency and remitted through a bank to Sri Lanka, are taxed at 15%. Gains and profits earned or derived from any foreign source, where such gains and profits are earned in foreign currency and remitted through a bank to Sri Lanka, are taxed at 15%. Special rates: gains and profits from conducting betting and gaming are taxed at 45%; gains and profits from manufacture and sale or import and sale of any liquor or tobacco products are taxed at 45%; gains from the realization of investment assets are taxed at 30%.

Remittance tax on a non-resident person is 14%. A 0% rate applies on remittances of retained income invested, if a non-resident person retained total income earned in Sri Lanka in any year of assessment commencing on or after 2021-04-01 for a minimum period of three years commencing from the first day of the immediately succeeding year of assessment in which the income is earned, and invested that income in Sri Lanka to expand its business, or to acquire shares or securities from the Colombo Stock Exchange licensed by the Securities and Exchange Commission of Sri Lanka, or to acquire any treasury bill, treasury bond, or Sri Lanka International Sovereign Bond issued on behalf of the Government of Sri Lanka.

subsection: Partnership taxable income and investment assets
text: A partnership is allowed to deduct any qualifying payments made during the year of assessment in computing its taxable income, and income tax is payable on taxable income. If taxable income does not exceed Rs. 1,000,000, the rate is 0%. If balance taxable income exceeds Rs. 1,000,000, the excess over Rs. 1,000,000 is taxed at 6%. Gains from realization of investment assets are taxed at 10%.

section: Tax Rates for Trusts and Unit Trusts or Mutual Funds
text: Taxable income of a trust is taxed at 30%, and gains from realization of investment assets by a trust are taxed at 10%. Taxable income of unit trusts or mutual funds, where section 59 applies, is taxed at 30%, and gains from realization of investment assets are taxed at 10%.

section: Tax Rates for NGOs, Charitable Institutions, and Employee-related Funds
text: Taxable income of a non-governmental organization is taxed at 30%; gains from realization of investment assets are taxed at 10%; and 30% applies on three percent of the amount received in a year of assessment by way of grant, donation, contribution, or in any other manner. Taxable income of charitable institutions is taxed at 14%, and gains from realization of investment assets are taxed at 10%. Taxable income of Employees Trust Funds, Provident or Pension Funds, and Termination Funds is taxed at 14%.

With effect from 2025-04-01, the page states that provisions of the Double Tax Avoidance Agreement between Sri Lanka and another country are applicable in respect of WHT on payments to a non-resident person. Rates listed: payment to a non-resident person with respect to land, sea, air transport or telecommunication service under section 85(2) of the Inland Revenue Act and Gazette Notification Extraordinary No. 2064/51 dated 2018-04-01 is 2%; from the sale price of any gem sold at an auction conducted by the National Gem and Jewellery Authority is 2.5%; service fee payments to a resident individual who is not an employee of the payer, if aggregate payment exceeds Rs. 100,000 per calendar month, are taxed at 5% on the full payment when the services are for teaching, lecturing, invigilating, or supervising an examination, as commission or brokerage to a resident insurance, sales, or canvassing agent, or for services provided by an independent service provider such as a doctor, engineer, accountant, lawyer, software developer, researcher, academic, or other prescribed individual service provider; interest or discount paid is taxed at 10%; rent payments to a resident person, if aggregate payment exceeds Rs. 100,000 per calendar month, are taxed at 10% on the full payment; an amount paid as winnings from a lottery, reward, betting, or gambling is taxed at 14%; charge, natural resource payment, or premium is taxed at 14%; royalty is taxed at 14%; rent payment to a non-resident person is taxed at 14%; service fee or insurance premium payments to a non-resident person are taxed at 14%; dividend is taxed at 15%.
Employers are required to remit APIT by deducting it from the remuneration of their respective employees. Due tax must be deducted in accordance with the APIT tables provided on the IRD web portal.

VAT registration threshold: if the total value of supplies exceeds or is likely to exceed Rs. 15 million for a taxable period of one month or three months, or Rs. 60 million in a twelve-month period, or if at any time there are reasonable grounds to believe the total value of supply will exceed the threshold, then with effect from 2024-10-01 the person shall be registered for VAT. The page also states that any person who carries out a taxable activity can register for VAT irrespective of the registration threshold. Standard VAT rate history on the page: 8% from 2019-12-01 to 2022-05-31; 12% from 2022-06-01 to 2022-08-31; 15% from 2022-09-01 to 2023-12-31; 18% from 2024-01-01. Direct export of goods and supply of certain services referred to in section 7 of the VAT Act are zero-rated at 0%. Supply of services by a hotel, guest house, restaurant, or other similar business registered with the Sri Lanka Tourism Development Authority, if not less than 60% of the total value of inputs are sourced locally, follows the same rate history: 8% from 2019-12-01 to 2022-05-31; 12% from 2022-06-01 to 2022-08-31; 15% from 2022-09-01 to 2023-12-31; 18% from 2024-01-01. Import of goods, namely fabrics set out in the H.S. Code and description specified in Columns I and II of the Schedule to Extraordinary Gazette Notification No. 2095/20 dated 2018-11-01, is taxed at 0%. VAT on financial services, calculated on value addition on financial services, was 15% up to 2021-12-31 and 18% with effect from 2022-01-01.

subsection: Registration thresholds, liable turnover, and rate
text: SSCL registration applies to persons whose aggregate turnover exceeded Rs. 60 million within the twelve months immediately prior to 2024-01-01, excluding turnover from exempted articles or services, or exceeds or is likely to exceed Rs. 15 million for any quarter commencing on or after 2024-01-01, excluding turnover from exempted articles or services. The page states that SSCL is applicable on the liable turnover. The table presents SSCL at 2.5% on liable turnover, with liable turnover defined as follows: importation of any article, 100% of turnover; manufacture of any article, 85% of turnover; supply of financial services, 100% of the value addition attributable to financial services referred to in the Gazette Notification issued under section 25C of the VAT Act; land and improvements, 100% of the liable turnover, defined as value of improvement equal to sale value minus market value of the bare land to the date of sale, referred to in section 5(7) of the Value Added Tax Act No. 14 of 2002; service other than the above, 100% of the turnover referred to in section 3(2)(c)(iii) of the Act; sale of any article by a registered distributor in relation to wholesale and retail sale, 25% of the turnover referred to in section 3(2)(d) of the Act; wholesale or retail sale other than the above, including importation and sale, 50% of the turnover referred to in section 3(2)(d) of the Act.

subsection: Affidavits, insurance, licenses, foreign card transactions, securities, and debt instruments
text: Stamp duty items listed on the page: any affidavit under Gazette Notification Extraordinary 1809/19 dated 2013-05-10, with effect from 2013-05-13, Rs. 50. Any policy of insurance, for every Rs. 1,000 or part thereof of the aggregate premia payable on the policy, under Gazette Notification Extraordinary 1789/9 dated 2012-12-17, with effect from 2013-01-01, Rs. 1. A warrant to act as a notary public, under Gazette Notification Extraordinary 1789/9 dated 2012-12-17, with effect from 2013-01-01, Rs. 2,000. Any license authorizing the holder to carry on any trade, business, profession, or vocation, other than a trade or business for the sale of liquor, for any period specified in the license, under Gazette Notification Extraordinary 2104/5 dated 2018-12-31, with effect from 2019-01-01, is charged at Rs. 2,000 or 10% of the license fee, whichever is lower. Any license authorizing the holder to carry on any trade or business for sale of liquor, for any period specified in the license, other than arrack licenses AFL1, AFL2, AFL3, and AFL4 issued together with foreign liquor licenses and licenses for the sale of bottled toddy, under the same gazette and effective date, is charged at Rs. 20,000. Arrack licenses AFL1, AFL2, AFL3, and AFL4 issued together with foreign liquor licenses and licenses for the sale of bottled toddy are charged at 2,000.00 or 10% of the license fee, whichever is higher, for each license. Any claim, demand, or request for payment of money due in respect of every credit card transaction entered into during any period commencing on or after 2016-01-01, where the merchant country is a country other than Sri Lanka, irrespective of card-present or card-not-present status and in any currency, for every Rs. 1,000 or part thereof of the sum of money, under Gazette Notification Extraordinary 1947/45 dated 2016-01-01, is charged at Rs. 25. Any share certificate issued consequent to the issue, transfer, or assignment of any number of shares of any company, for every Rs. 1,000 or part thereof of the aggregate value of such shares as assessed for open market value on the relevant date, under Gazette Notification Extraordinary 1882/17 dated 2014-09-30 and 1864/2 dated 2014-05-26, with effect from 2014-05-26, is charged at Rs. 5. Any bond or mortgage for a definite and certain sum of money affecting property, for every Rs. 1,000 or part thereof, under Gazette Notification Extraordinary 1933/14 dated 2015-09-21, with effect from 2015-09-21, is charged at Rs. 1. Any promissory note, for every Rs. 1,000 or part thereof, under Gazette Notification Extraordinary 1465/19 dated 2006-10-05, with effect from 2006-10-05, is charged at Rs. 1.

subsection: Lease, hire, hire purchase, receipts, and discharges
text: For any instrument relating to the lease or hire of any property, stamp duty is charged at Rs. 20 for every Rs. 1,000 or part thereof of the aggregate lease or hire, including any premium, payable for the whole term comprised in the lease or hire agreement, other than a hire purchase agreement. Where the term exceeds twenty years, the aggregate hire or lease payable for the whole term is deemed to be the aggregate payable for the first twenty years of the term. This is stated under Gazette Notification Extraordinary 1465/19 dated 2006-10-05, with effect from 2006-10-05. For every Rs. 1,000 or part thereof of the aggregate amount payable under a hire purchase agreement entered into under the Consumer Credit Act No. 29 of 1982, the stamp duty is Rs. 20, under the same gazette and effective date. For any receipt or discharge given for any money or property, amounts up to and including Rs. 25,000 are exempt under Gazette Notification Extraordinary 1530/13 dated 2008-01-01 with effect from 2008-01-01, and amounts above Rs. 25,000 are charged Rs. 25.
Share Transaction Levy is charged at 0.3% on the turnover of both the buyer and the seller.

subsection: Betting, telecast, casino, entrance levy, and bookmaker tax
text: Betting and Gaming Levy rates listed on the page: betting business done through agents, for every year commencing on or after 2015-04-01, Rs. 4,000,000. If live telecast facilities are issued, for every year commencing on or after 2015-04-01, Rs. 600,000. If live telecast facilities are not used, for every year commencing on or after 2015-04-01, Rs. 50,000. Business of gaming: business of gaming (casino), per place of business, for the issue or renewal of a license for five years, with effect from 2022-08-31, Rs. 500,000,000. Annual fee for business of gaming (casino), per place of business, with effect from 2023-04-01, Rs. 500,000,000. Casino entrance levy: a person carrying on the business of gaming in Sri Lanka shall collect USD 50 or equivalent from any person who enters the place of business of gaming. Business of bookmaker: tax on the gross collection of a bookmaker or gaming business, after deducting winning prizes, where the gross collection exceeds Rs. 1,000,000 per month, is 15%.
